title: npmPackages omits several npm-installable artifacts
reasoning: AgentSense, AgentTK, and Starglass present npm install commands, but none set npmName. The exported npmPackages list is derived only from npmName, so any registry/status consumer of that export will silently exclude these npm-installable tools.
recommendation: Add npmName for every artifact that should be represented as an npm package, or rename/narrow npmPackages so it is clear it only includes a hand-selected subset.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current tests validate tool detail generation, selected links, and demo media, but they do not assert package identity consistency between install commands and npmPackages.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a config test that every artifact with a real 'npm install <package>' command either has npmName set to that package or is explicitly excluded by a documented flag.
minimumFixScope: Update src/config/packages.ts metadata and add a focused package identity consistency test.

history.note: Implemented in working tree; native checks pass. Full Clawpatch provider revalidation was curtailed after confirming first five fixes because it was too slow.
